entities.name: Thai Union Group Public Company Limited

financial.note: Collaborates on global food distribution and joint ventures in functional ingredients.
TU completed its exit strategy in early 2026; the removal of this loss-making 'minority investment' has significantly de-risked the balance sheet.

supply_chain.note: Prices reached $2,000/ton in March 2026; high costs are expected to impact margins starting 3Q26 once low-cost inventory is depleted.
Ensures vertical integration for aquaculture and pet food business segments.
Key partner for shrimp feed and processing in the South Asian market.

equity_cross_holdings.note: The group's high-margin growth engine; ITC's record purchase orders drive TU's 2026 performance.

competitors.note: Rivalry in the global frozen seafood and processed meat sectors.
Global competitor in value-added seafood and aquaculture.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: TU implemented price adjustments in 1Q26 to mitigate the impact of rising tariffs on its processed seafood exports.
As a global exporter, a weaker Baht bolsters translated THB revenue and overall competitiveness.
TU's ฿9bn 'Blue Bond' (2026) ties financing costs to SeaChange® 2030 sustainability KPIs.
